title: Web Application Security
description: Build durable understanding of common security risks in modern web applications.

lessons.title: Input Validation
lessons.summary: Treat all external input as untrusted until validated.
content: Every field, query string, header, cookie, and uploaded file should be treated as untrusted input.
Client-side validation improves usability, but server-side validation is the true security boundary.
Validation should check type, length, format, allowed values, and business rules.

practice.question: What is the safest default assumption for user-provided data?
options: It is trusted if it came from your UI
It is safe if JavaScript checked it first
practice.explanation: All external input should be treated as untrusted until validated on the server.

practice.question: Which validation boundary actually protects the application?
practice.explanation: Client-side checks are easy to bypass. Server-side validation is the real enforcement point.

lessons.title: SQL Injection
lessons.summary: Unsafe query construction allows attackers to change query logic.
content: SQL injection happens when user input is inserted into queries without safe parameterization.
Prepared statements separate data from executable SQL logic.
Input validation helps, but parameterized queries are essential.

practice.question: What is the primary defense against SQL injection?
practice.explanation: Parameterized queries prevent user input from changing SQL structure.

reviewCards.front: What is the golden rule for data received by a web application?
reviewCards.back: Treat all incoming data as untrusted or invalid until it passes the right validation checks.

reviewCards.front: Which validation boundary actually protects the application?
What is the primary defense against SQL injection?
